_comment: Auto-play (interactive screenshot test) policy. Controls how many click steps the auto-play test runs, per-trigger. Phase 0d.20 (2026-04-30) extracted from hardcoded Swift constants per CLAUDE.md RULE #1. Edit + push + (relaunch OR refreshMchataisourceCache) ships changes with no Swift rebuild.

maxStepsByTrigger._evaluatorRunComment: When the user explicitly clicks Evaluate. Bumped from 2 → 4: the original 2 only clicked 2 buttons, missing bugs in the 3rd-Nth interaction (e.g. 'Add 3 timers, then click Reset' fails on click 4). 4 covers the typical 'add some items, then trigger an action' workflow.
maxStepsByTrigger._postGenerationAutoComment: Fired automatically after generation completes. Same as evaluatorRun: enough to exercise add-then-action sequences.
maxStepsByTrigger._postFixCycleComment: After an auto-fix re-generation. Lower because we just fixed something specific; if fix is good it'll show in 3 clicks.

_screenshotIntervalComment: Wait between click and afterScreenshot capture. Lets DOM update fire (setInterval/setTimeout-based timers, animations) before we judge if the screen 'changed'. 800ms is a good middle: long enough for transitions, short enough to keep total auto-play time bounded.

consoleErrorPolicy._comment: Phase 0d.21 — console.error / window.onerror captured during auto-play. Surfaces JS bugs invisible to screenshot.
consoleErrorPolicy._failOnAnyErrorComment: When true: any uncaught error during auto-play step → mark step FAIL with the error message. When false: errors are reported but don't fail the step (use during initial rollout to gather data).
consoleErrorPolicy._ignorePatternsComment: Substring matches; harmless errors that fire on unrelated browser internals. Keep this list short.

autoFixPolicy._comment: When auto-play finds bugs, what to do. Phase 0d.18 dropped the skipAutoFix gate; this is the policy for the autofix that fires.
autoFixPolicy._maxAutoFixCyclesComment: How many fix-and-retest rounds. After this we surface to the user as 'auto-test still finds issues' rather than looping forever.
autoFixPolicy._fixPromptVariantComment: Tells the autofix LLM to anchor on the ORIGINAL_GOAL so a 'fix the New Game button' prompt doesn't drift the app into a card game (real-incident 2026-04-29).

keyboardSynthesis._comment: Phase 0d.27 (2026-04-30) — when the goal mentions a movement-based game (snake/tetris/platformer/etc), getClickableElements() misses the directional controls (they're keyboard/swipe-driven, not DOM buttons). Auto-play falls back to synthesizing keyboard events to exercise those controls. Real-incident: snake game's movement was keyboard-only and auto-play only clicked Pause+Play Again, so movement bugs were invisible.
keyboardSynthesis._triggerKeywordsComment: Substring match on the goal text (case-insensitive). Add new arcade types here without rebuild.
keyboardSynthesis._keysToTryComment: Tried in this order. ArrowUp/Down/Left/Right covers most snake/frogger/pacman. Space covers jump/shoot. WASD covers fallback platformer schemes. Each key is dispatched and screen is checked for change (just like clicks).
keyboardSynthesis._screenshotIntervalMsComment: Wait between key event and screenshot. Shorter than click-interval because keypresses on game loops typically settle in <500ms (one game tick).
keyboardSynthesis._maxKeysPerStepComment: After click steps, fire this many keyboard events. Smaller than click maxSteps because the goal isn't to play through — it's to verify directional input is wired.
